explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the pilot is in control (standby), it should eventually transition to the STANDBY state. The chosen template is a response to a change in state of the autopilot, and ensures the autopilot transitions to standby within a certain window of time. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition for the autopilot to be in the TRANSITION state. The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition for the autopilot to be in the STANDBY state.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot shall transition to STANDBY when the pilot is in control.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the STANDBY state. It uses a combination of the 'G' (always) and 'F' (eventually) temporal operators, ensuring that the transition happens whenever the pilot is in control.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the STANDBY state. The property uses the 'G' operator to indicate that this must always hold true, and the 'F' operator to indicate that the transition to the STANDBY state will happen eventually. The 'N_DURATION' parameter is set to 1, indicating that the transition must happen within one time step after the pilot takes control.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the STANDBY state. The property uses a 'G' operator to indicate that this behavior must hold globally, and an 'F' operator to indicate that the transition to the STANDBY state must eventually occur. The property also includes the condition "(! state_is_STANDBY) & (X state_is_STANDBY)" to ensure that the autopilot is indeed transitioning from the TRANSITION state to the STANDBY state.

The LTL formula states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true) and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION is true), then in the next state the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY is true). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from TRANSITION to STANDBY when the pilot is in control.

This is expressed using a "G" operator (always) to ensure that the implication holds for all time. The implication checks that if the pilot is in control (standby is true), then the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state from the TRANSITION state.  The "X" operator (next) indicates that this transition should occur in the next time step.
